name: Rakhine
notes: Rakhine / Arakanese (ရခိုင်) — Burmish (Sino-Tibetan) language of Rakhine State, Myanmar, closely related to Burmese and conservative in retaining /r/. Written in the Mon–Burmese script (Burmese alphabet), a Brahmic abugida: consonant letters carry an inherent vowel modified by vowel signs, medial signs and a virama (asat) for stacking and coda marking. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: Burmese-script spelling is highly etymological and encodes tone through combinations of vowel signs and diacritics, so it needs a dedicated abugida model. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Burmese script (Unicode block Myanmar).
